title: ASIC opens fire on former Rex execs
full_text:
A court has heard Rex Airlines had just recorded one of its worst months on record when the then executive chairman signed off on a statement telling the market it was on track for an operating profit in the 2023 financial year.
The Australian Securities and Investments Commission has accused the airline of misleading and deceptive conduct, and alleges four former directors failed in their duties of continuous disclosure.
Opening ASIC’s case in the NSW Supreme Court on Monday, Michael Borsky KC said the case centred on a statement issued on February 28, 2023, which misrepresented Rex’s true financial position.
Mr Borsky said it was alleged former executive chairman Lim Kim Hai allowed the company to publish the guidance without reasonable grounds, and failed to withdraw or correct it.
In addition, former deputy chairman John Sharp and directors Lincoln Pan and Siddharth Khotkar are accused of breaching their duty of care.
Judge Ashley Black heard the statement in question came amid a situation where profits were not enough to place Rex in a “break even” position. Against this backdrop, it’s alleged Mr Lim still found fit to issue guidance forecasting an operating profit for the full 2023 financial year. Rex was placed into administration in July 2024.
summary: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) is taking legal action against former executives of Rex Airlines for allegedly misleading the market about the company's financial health in early 2023. Four former directors, including the executive chairman, are accused of failing to disclose the true financial situation before the airline went into administration in July 2024.
heading: Regulation
key_points: ASIC accuses Rex Airlines of misleading conduct.
Former chairman Lim Kim Hai signed off on false profit guidance.
Four directors, including John Sharp, face legal action.
Rex Airlines went into administration in July 2024.
ticker_line: ASIC targets Rex Airlines' former executives
